title: JoyConRightGamepadMapping
description: Mapping for the Nintendo Joy-Con (R) held horizontally as a solo controller. Declares only channels that physically exist on the device — one stick (mapped to the LeftStick channels to match the W3C standard layout for the lone reported stick regardless of which Joy-Con reports it), four face buttons, the SL/SR inner shoulders (routed through the standard shoulder channels), Plus, the Home button, and the stick-click. Right-stick channels, triggers, Minus/Capture, Touchpad, paddles, and auxiliary axes are intentionally absent. Use GamepadMapping.hasChannel to detect availability before binding inputs that may not exist on every device family.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: Mapping for the Nintendo Joy-Con (R) held horizontally as a solo controller.

Declares only channels that physically exist on the device — one stick (mapped to the LeftStick channels to match the W3C standard layout for the lone reported stick regardless of which Joy-Con reports it), four face buttons, the SL/SR inner shoulders (routed through the standard shoulder channels), Plus, the Home button, and the stick-click.

Right-stick channels, triggers, Minus/Capture, Touchpad, paddles, and auxiliary axes are intentionally absent. Use GamepadMapping.hasChannel to detect availability before binding inputs that may not exist on every device family.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Releases all button and axis control references held by this mapping. Call when the associated gamepad disconnects to allow garbage collection.

members.name: hasChannel
members.description: Returns true when this mapping declares at least one button or axis control that writes to channel. Use to detect device-specific capabilities at runtime — e.g. before binding an input to a right-stick channel that may not exist on a single Joy-Con.

members.name: axes
members.description: Ordered list of axes, indexed by the Gamepad API axis index.

members.name: buttons
members.description: Ordered list of buttons, indexed by the Gamepad API button index.

members.name: family
members.description: Identifies the device family this mapping targets.
